methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $129M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
GITAI, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $129M+ to date, was valued using two triangulation methods against a comparable universe of nine same-sector peers spanning Series B through Series D stages. The peer set includes both private companies (Hadrian at $1.6B, 1X Technologies at $10.0B, Exotec at $2.0B, Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B, Dexterity at $1.6B) and one public comparable, with valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B.

Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $129M raised proxy, implying a $219M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company robotics cohort yielded a $1.6B midpoint valuation, reflecting the elevated funding environment and growth trajectory in the sector. These methods produce materially different outputs, reflecting the wide dispersion in peer valuations and the stage-dependent nature of robotics company multiples.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $186M (conservative) to $1.8B (peer-anchored), with a median of $910M. Key uncertainties include GITAI's relative revenue scale and profitability relative to the peer set, the composition bias introduced by the high outlier (1X at $10.0B), and whether near-term financing or operational milestones would compress or widen this range.
